DSP-Z8IVAT.desc: The DSP-Z8 IV AT is an 8-channel standalone digital signal processor featuring a CS47048 32-bit/192 kHz DSP and auto-calibration (AutoTune) with optional microphone. It accepts up to 8 channels of speaker-level or RCA analog input plus coaxial and optical digital inputs, and outputs 8 channels of 5 V RMS RCA signal. A port for the optional HD-BT module adds aptX HD Bluetooth streaming, and the included LCD dash remote provides source, volume, and bass control.
narr: Key Features
CS47048 32-bit/192 kHz DSP with THD+N −98 dB
AutoTune auto-calibration with optional M-AT1 calibration microphone
Coaxial (32-bit/192 kHz) and optical (24-bit/192 kHz) digital inputs
8-channel speaker-level inputs (2–20 V) with auto-on
Port for optional HD-BT II-D module — aptX / aptX HD Bluetooth streaming
Included 1" LCD dash remote with source, volume, and bass output control
sections: 0 – 15 ms, step 0.02 ms (max 515 cm, step 0.68 cm)
aptX HD via optional HD-BT II-D module

ADSP-Z12IV-10A.desc: The ADSP-Z12 IV-10A is an integrated 12-channel DSP and 10-channel amplifier delivering 10 × 120 W at 2 Ω or 10 × 80 W at 4 Ω. The CS47048 32-bit/192 kHz DSP core provides 31-band graphic/parametric equalization, four crossover filter types, and time alignment across 12 processing channels. Coaxial and optical digital inputs, 8-channel speaker-level inputs with auto-on, and a port for the optional HD-BT module round out the connectivity.
narr: Key Features
10-channel amplifier: 10 × 120 W @ 2 Ω / 10 × 80 W @ 4 Ω
CS47048 32-bit/192 kHz DSP with THD+N −98 dB
31-band graphic/parametric EQ with Linkwitz, Butterworth, Bessel, Tchebyschev crossovers
Coaxial (32-bit/192 kHz) and optical (24-bit/192 kHz) digital inputs
8-channel speaker-level inputs (2–20 V) with auto-on
Port for optional HD-BT II-D module — aptX / aptX HD Bluetooth streaming
sections: 0 – 15 ms, step 0.02 ms (max 515 cm, step 0.68 cm)
aptX HD via optional HD-BT II-D module

ADSP-Z16IV-12A.desc: The ADSP-Z16 IV-12A is an integrated 16-channel DSP and 12-channel amplifier delivering 12 × 120 W at 2 Ω or 12 × 80 W at 4 Ω. Built on the same CS47048 32-bit/192 kHz DSP platform as the rest of the DSP-IV series, it supports 12 channels of speaker-level input (2–20 V) with auto-on, 8-channel RCA input, and 4-channel RCA output, along with coaxial and optical digital inputs. The optional HD-BT module adds aptX HD Bluetooth streaming.
narr: Key Features
12-channel amplifier: 12 × 120 W @ 2 Ω / 12 × 80 W @ 4 Ω
CS47048 32-bit/192 kHz DSP with THD+N −98 dB
31-band graphic/parametric EQ with Linkwitz, Butterworth, Bessel, Tchebyschev crossovers
12-channel speaker-level inputs (2–20 V) with auto-on
Coaxial (32-bit/192 kHz) and optical (24-bit/192 kHz) digital inputs
Port for optional HD-BT II-D module — aptX / aptX HD Bluetooth streaming
sections: 0 – 15 ms, step 0.02 ms (max 515 cm, step 0.68 cm)
aptX HD via optional HD-BT II-D module

Z-CB100F.desc: The Z-CB100F is a 100 Farad supercapacitor bank built from six 600 Farad supercapacitors connected in series to deliver 100 F (100,000,000 µF) of power reserve capacity. Its extremely low internal resistance versus conventional electrolytic capacitors provides a very fast transient response that Zapco states greatly improves sound quality. Integrated voltage and current indicators allow monitoring of operating parameters across three or more amplifiers.
narr: Key Features
100 Farad (100,000,000 µF) total capacitance
Built from 6 × 600 Farad supercapacitors connected in series
Ultra-low internal resistance for fast transient response
Integrated voltage and current indicators for system monitoring
Supports monitoring operating parameters for 3 or more amplifiers

intro: The Zapco DSP-IV Series brings audiophile-grade digital signal processing to an accessible price point — a lineage that traces back to the original DSP-Z8 IV, which Zapco credits with performance superior to competing units costing twice as much and even earning use in top-tier Master Class SQ competition vehicles. The current generation, the DSP-IV AT, refines that legacy with a sharp new black-and-copper aesthetic matching the AP amplifier family, adding coaxial digital input alongside optical, and introducing the AutoTune auto-calibration function for AT-designated models.

The series spans a standalone 8-channel processor (DSP-Z8 IV AT) and three integrated DSP/amplifier units (ADSP-Z8 IV-6AT, ADSP-Z12 IV-10A, ADSP-Z16 IV-12A). All models share the CS47048 32-bit/192 kHz DSP engine, 31-band graphic/parametric EQ, four crossover filter families, per-channel time alignment up to 515 cm, and a port for the optional HD-BT II-D module providing aptX HD Bluetooth streaming. The GUI's Advanced menu adds a Mixing Set for manual input-to-channel routing and, on AT units, the AutoTune function that automatically sets signal delay and channel phase using an optional calibration microphone.
